So my first year of selling when I was at G2, and that was my third year of my career.

So you can't call them for references.
So then you're not stacking your work.
You're not able to multiply later based on your network of a reference or a referral or a recommendation.
If you want to go to a new job, I ended up changing my approach completely from that and made my manager a little mad at the time.
But because it takes a while to like build up that kind of credibility where you're not trying to sell to everybody.
So I was one of the people that was saying no to people on the phone of like, don't buy this, go buy our competitor because that's what you're looking for.
And as I became more honest with people of just what the options were that they had and
I actually told them things that a lot of sellers are scared to say because it might lose them the deal.
That's what ultimately brings you trust.
So then after that year, that was when I really started to get a really big network of customers that trusted me.
And I could call about, you know, some became friends, some are just acquaintances, but I still carry a lot of them to this day.
think I have a little over 30 customers that have written me recommendations on when Bravado was doing this.
